about/community.title: Welcome with a *coconut hug.*
about/community.lede: Meet the humans behind CocoIndex on Discord, GitHub, and beyond, and find the channels where issues, ideas, and pull requests all get a warm reply.

ai/llm.title: Plug in *any LLM.*
ai/llm.lede: One LlmSpec unlocks OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Bedrock, and more, for text generation and embeddings inside your flows.

cocoinsight_access.title: Open your flow to *CocoInsight.*
cocoinsight_access.lede: Start the local HTTP server, point the CocoInsight web UI at it, and inspect every flow schema and row without ever shipping your data off-box.

contributing/guide.title: Contribute to *CocoIndex.*
contributing/guide.lede: From good first issues to pull requests: how to claim work, run prek checks, and get your change merged into the main branch within a business day.

contributing/new_built_in_target.title: Ship a *new built-in target.*
contributing/new_built_in_target.lede: Implement TargetFactoryBase in Rust to wire a new destination into CocoIndex, handling setup changes, mutations, and schema with full access to flow metadata.

contributing/setup_dev_environment.title: Set up the *dev loop.*
contributing/setup_dev_environment.lede: Install Rust, uv, and prek hooks, build the native extension with maturin develop, and get a local CocoIndex checkout ready to hack on.

core/basics.title: How *indexing* works.
core/basics.lede: Flows, data scopes, operations, and incremental updates: the mental model you need before writing your first transform. Schema is known up front, data stays observable end to end.

core/cli.title: Drive flows from the *command line.*
core/cli.lede: The cocoindex CLI points at an APP_TARGET and takes over setup, update, evaluate, and server commands, with .env discovery and global options ready to go.

core/cli-commands.title: Every *cocoindex* subcommand.
core/cli-commands.lede: A complete reference for drop, evaluate, setup, show, update, server, and friends, with every flag and what it does.

core/data_types.title: The CocoIndex *type system.*
core/data_types.lede: Basic types, structs, KTables, LTables, and vectors, all inferred at flow-definition time and mapped to the right Python annotations when you cross the boundary.

core/flow_def.title: Define a *flow.*
core/flow_def.lede: Compose sources, transforms, collectors, and exports by wiring data slices through a FlowBuilder. The shape of your index falls out of the code you write.

core/flow_methods.title: Operate a *live flow.*
core/flow_methods.lede: Once a flow is defined, use the CLI or Python API to set up backends, run one-shot updates, stream live changes, or evaluate without touching the target.

core/settings.title: Configure *CocoIndex.*
core/settings.lede: Point CocoIndex at your database, pick an app namespace, and tune process-level settings through environment variables, a settings function, or explicit init.

custom_ops/custom_functions.title: Write your own *transform.*
custom_ops/custom_functions.lede: Drop in a standalone Python function for quick work, or pair a spec with an executor for caching, GPU loads, and setup logic that runs once per operation.

custom_ops/custom_sources.title: Bring your own *data source.*
custom_ops/custom_sources.lede: Implement create, list, and get_value on a source connector to stream any API, database, or filesystem into a flow with ordinals for incremental updates.

custom_ops/custom_targets.title: Export to *anywhere you want.*
custom_ops/custom_targets.lede: Pair a target spec with a connector that handles setup changes and mutations, and CocoIndex will drive insert, update, and delete traffic for you.

getting_started/installation.title: Install CocoIndex *and Postgres.*
getting_started/installation.lede: pip install cocoindex, start pgvector in Docker, and confirm Python 3.11 to 3.13 on macOS, Linux, or Windows. Three minutes from zero to a working local stack.

getting_started/overview.title: Real-time data, *incrementally.*
getting_started/overview.lede: CocoIndex is an ultra-performant ETL framework for AI: dataflow programming, observable lineage, and incremental processing that only touches what actually changed.

getting_started/quickstart.title: Build your first flow in *10 minutes.*
getting_started/quickstart.lede: Point CocoIndex at a folder of markdown files, chunk and embed each doc with a sentence transformer, and land the vectors in Postgres with pgvector, end to end.

ops/functions.title: Built-in *functions.*
ops/functions.lede: ParseJson, SplitRecursively, EmbedText, ExtractByLlm, DetectProgrammingLanguage, and the rest of the standard library of transforms you can drop into any flow.

query.title: Query your *index.*
query.lede: Share embedding logic between indexing and search via transform flows, then register query handlers so CocoInsight and your app can hit the same code path.

sources.title: Where your *data comes from.*
sources.lede: LocalFile, AmazonS3, AzureBlob, GoogleDrive, and Postgres: the built-in sources that feed rows into a flow, each with its own change-capture story.

sources/amazons3.title: Index an *S3 bucket.*
sources/amazons3.lede: Point CocoIndex at an S3 bucket with IAM credentials, filter by prefix and glob patterns, and wire up an SQS queue for near-instant change notifications.

sources/azureblob.title: Index *Azure Blob Storage.*
sources/azureblob.lede: Connect with a SAS token, an access key, or default Azure credentials and stream blobs from a container into a flow, with include and exclude glob patterns.

sources/googledrive.title: Index a *Google Drive* folder.
sources/googledrive.lede: Authenticate with a service account, share the target folders, and CocoIndex will import files and poll for recent changes without re-listing the whole tree.

sources/localfile.title: Index *local files.*
sources/localfile.lede: Point at a directory, set glob include and exclude patterns, optionally enable watch_changes, and CocoIndex turns the filesystem into a KTable of filename and content.

sources/postgres.title: Index a *Postgres table.*
sources/postgres.lede: Pull rows from any primary-keyed table, filter with a SQL WHERE expression, and use LISTEN/NOTIFY or an ordinal column to catch every insert, update, and delete.

targets.title: Where your *index lives.*
targets.lede: Postgres, Qdrant, LanceDB, Pinecone, ChromaDB, Doris, Neo4j, Ladybug: pick the vector, relational, or graph store that fits your retrieval workload.

targets/chromadb.title: Export to *ChromaDB.*
targets/chromadb.lede: Map each collected row to a ChromaDB document with one embedding vector, route a chosen field into the document body, and let the rest land as metadata.

targets/doris.title: Export to *Apache Doris.*
targets/doris.lede: Write rows to Doris tables with vector indexes and full-text search, using Stream Load for bulk ingest and the DUPLICATE KEY model required by Doris 4.0+.

targets/kuzu.title: Kuzu has moved to *Ladybug.*
targets/kuzu.lede: The Kuzu target was retired. Use the Ladybug target instead, a maintained fork that carries forward Kuzu's embedded property-graph model under an MIT license.

targets/ladybug.title: Export to *Ladybug* graphs.
targets/ladybug.lede: Talk to a Ladybug API server over HTTP, map collected rows to nodes or relationships, and get an embedded property-graph target with lakehouse-friendly tooling.

targets/lancedb.title: Export to *LanceDB.*
targets/lancedb.lede: Write rows to a LanceDB table with an automatic B-Tree primary-key index, optional FTS indexes, and periodic optimize() calls tuned by a transaction threshold.

targets/neo4j.title: Export to *Neo4j* graphs.
targets/neo4j.lede: Map collected rows to nodes and relationships in a Neo4j database, declare extra labels for dangling endpoints, and let CocoIndex deduplicate by primary key.

targets/pinecone.title: Export to *Pinecone.*
targets/pinecone.lede: Stream collected rows as Pinecone vectors with a single primary key, pick a cloud and region for serverless indexes, and batch upserts with one tunable parameter.

targets/postgres.title: Export to *Postgres* with pgvector.
targets/postgres.lede: Land rows in a Postgres table with pgvector columns for fixed-dimension embeddings, per-column options, and optional custom schemas and table names.

targets/qdrant.title: Export to *Qdrant.*
targets/qdrant.lede: Each collected row becomes a Qdrant point, with fixed-dimension vectors mapped to named dense or multi-vectors and everything else folded into the payload.

tutorials/control_flow.title: Tune *concurrency* without blowing up memory.
tutorials/control_flow.lede: Cap rows and bytes in flight at the global, per-source, and per-row layer so CocoIndex stays fast on millions of items without melting your machine.

tutorials/docker_pgvector_setup.title: Run Postgres with *pgvector* locally.
tutorials/docker_pgvector_setup.lede: A step-by-step Docker setup for pgvector, a Python virtualenv, a text-embedding flow, and a semantic-search query, with the common port and extension gotchas called out.

tutorials/live_updates.title: Keep your index *always fresh.*
tutorials/live_updates.lede: Combine refresh intervals with per-source change capture, from S3 SQS notifications to Google Drive polling, and let FlowLiveUpdater keep the target in sync.

tutorials/manage_flow_dynamically.title: Spin up *flows on demand.*
tutorials/manage_flow_dynamically.lede: Parameterize a flow definition with open_flow(), drive multiple instances from a config registry, and handle setup, update, and close for each one on the fly.
